Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-General, Set One And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,697.00
Off Calendar Aug-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. James Dal Bon, Esq. of Wisdom Law Group APC (tel: 408-915-3700) for the Plaintiff.
Atty. Michael Kim, Esq. of CKB Vienna LLP (tel: 909-980-1040) for the Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT TRITON GLOBAL SERVICES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-General, Set One And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,697.00.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Triton Global Services, Inc.'s motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories, set one, and for sanctions is granted as stated herein.

Plaintiff Alan Zer's responses (including the amended responses dated July 30, 2025) are incomplete or evasive and shall be amended as to form interrogatory nos. 2.6, 2.7, 8.4, 8.7, 12.1, 17.1 (as to each individual RFA that was other than an unqualified admission). Zer cannot provide information that he does not recall or does not have, but the responses should make clear that Zer has made an inquiry and that the answer is based on his best available information. Zer shall amend by September 5, 2025.

Triton seeks sanctions. Code of Civil Procedure, section 2030.290, subdivision (c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to interrogatories . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." Zer's opposition lacks substantial justification but the court reduces the sanction amount in light of all the circumstances. The court orders Zer to pay $1000 to Triton within 30 days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,816.50; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Michael I. Kim In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Aug-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. James Dal Bon, Esq. of Wisdom Law Group APC (tel: 408-915-3700) for the Plaintiff.
Atty. Michael Kim, Esq. of CKB Vienna LLP (tel: 909-980-1040) for the Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT TRITON GLOBAL SERVICES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,816.50.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Triton Global Services, Inc.'s motion to compel further responses to requests for production and for sanctions is granted as stated herein.

Plaintiff Alan Zer did not timely respond to the discovery and thereby waived his objections. (Code Civ. Proc., section 2031.300 subd. (a).) A party seeking relief from waiver of objections must do so by noticed motion. (Id. ["The court, on motion, may relieve that party from this waiver . . . ."].) In any event, Zer's counsel's declaration in support of the opposition to this motion presents only hearsay as to Zer's hardships and offers no reason why timely objections could not have been made. Moreover, the responses are not in substantial compliance with the discovery statutes.

For some RFPs, Zer's statement of compliance represented that he would produce nonprivileged documents. Zer has not moved for relief from waiver and has waived this objection. The court orders Zer to provide further responses to RFP Nos. 1, 3-9, 11, 15, 19, 22, 25, 26, 28-31, 33-38, 40, 45, 47, 51, 53-55. Zer shall respond either with a complete statement of compliance or a statement of inability to comply. For the following RFPs, Zer has not asserted objections but his statement of compliance is ambiguous; Zer shall amend to provide a complete statement of compliance or inability to comply: Nos. 17, 21, 23, 24, 27, 32, 39, 41, 43, 44, 46, 48, 52. Zer shall amend by September 5, 2025.

The court has reviewed the supplemental responses to the requests for production. They do not contain code-complaint statements of compliance or inability to comply and therefore do not cure the issues raised by this motion.

Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, section 2031.320, subdivision (a), the court orders Zer to produce documents in accordance with his statement of compliance by September 2, 2025. The court understands that Zer made a supplemental production after the motion was filed but has objected to producing certain documents before a protective order is entered. Because Zer did not timely respond to the discovery, this objection is waived.

Code of Civil Procedure, section 2031.300, subdivision (c), provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to a demand for inspection . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." Zer's opposition lacks substantial justification but the court reduces the sanction amount in light of all the circumstances. The court orders Zer to pay $1000 to Triton within 30 days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, Set One And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,223.00
Off Calendar Aug-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. James Dal Bon, Esq. of Wisdom Law Group APC (tel: 408-915-3700) for the Plaintiff.
Atty. Michael Kim, Esq. of CKB Vienna LLP (tel: 909-980-1040) for the Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT TRITON GLOBAL SERVICES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, Set One And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,223.00.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Triton Global Services, Inc.'s motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories - employment, set one, and for sanctions is granted as stated herein.

Plaintiff Alan Zer's responses (including the amended responses dated July 31, 2025) are incomplete or evasive and shall be amended as to form interrogatory - employment nos. 202.1(e) (no documents attached or provided), 203.1(d) (response is too general), and 203.1(e) (response is incomplete as to who has knowledge of the facts). Zer shall provide further responses by September 5, 2025. The court otherwise denies relief.

Triton seeks sanctions. Code of Civil Procedure, section 2030.290, subdivision (c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to interrogatories . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." Zer cured some of the defects on which Triton initially moved in his amendment, but the service of amended responses does not deprive the court of authority to issue sanctions. (Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 390, 396.) Here, the initial responses were deficient and Zer cured some (but not all) of the defects after Triton's efforts to meet and confer and file a motion. The court finds this is a misuse of the discovery process and awards $1000 sanctions in light of all the circumstances.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Respnses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,104.50
Off Calendar Aug-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. James Dal Bon, Esq. of Wisdom Law Group APC (tel: 408-915-3700) for the Plaintiff.
Atty. Michael Kim, Esq. of CKB Vienna LLP (tel: 909-980-1040) for the Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT MAAZ SHAIKH's Motion To Compel Further Respnses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,104.50.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Maaz Shaikh's motion to compel further responses to requests for production and for sanctions is granted as stated herein.

Plaintiff Alan Zer did not timely respond to the discovery and thereby waived his objections. (Code Civ. Proc., section 2031.300 subd. (a).) A party seeking relief from waiver of objections must do so by noticed motion. (Id. ["The court, on motion, may relieve that party from this waiver . . . ."].) In any event, Zer's counsel's declaration in support of the opposition to this motion presents only hearsay as to Zer's hardships and offers no reason why timely objections could not have been made. Moreover, the responses are not in substantial compliance with the discovery statutes.

For RFPs 1-17, Zer's original response stated that no documents existed. His amended responses dated July 31, 2025, identify a police report, medical records, and a right to sue letter as the only responsive documents for each of those RFPs. Neither set of responses included a code-compliant statement of compliance or inability to comply. Zer shall provide further responses to RFP Nos. 1-17 that include either a statement of compliance or a statement of inability to comply. For RFP 18, if Zer identifies any new documents in his amended response to form interrogatory 17.1, he shall provide it; otherwise, the response is sufficient.

Code of Civil Procedure, section 2031.300, subdivision (c), provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to a demand for inspection . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." Zer's opposition lacks substantial justification but the court reduces the sanction amount in light of all the circumstances. The court orders Zer to pay $1000 to Shaikh within 30 days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
